modelStrategy: DeepSeek-first generation plus DeepSeek quality repair; script performs mechanical validation/rendering only

qualityReview.summary: Mechanical validation passed, but pedagogical and renderer review found quality issues that need DeepSeek repair.
issues: stemParts currently use {text: ...} instead of {type:'text', value:'...'}, causing question stems to render blank in the review HTML.

Several figure answerLock objects are empty. Each figure must contain the values students are expected to infer, such as equal/opposite relationships, bearing angles, vector directions, or component magnitudes.
Some figureSpec arrow coordinates are outside the renderer viewBox safe area. Use x=40..680 and y=50..330 for arrows, labels y=30..350.

The cube figure is pedagogically risky: the EF/AB/FB/AE relationships may not match the conventional cube naming used in class. If using a cube, define the layout clearly in figureSpec and make answerLock explicit; otherwise replace with a simpler comparison_vectors diagram.
Previous repair failed because fig1 answerLock marked the same vector pairs as both equal and opposite. This is a concept blocker.

Q10 says true bearing 120° equals S60°E, but 120° clockwise from north is E30°S or S60°E depending convention; the prompt asks compass quadrants and needs clearer wording and answerLock to avoid ambiguity.

The card overuses isolated recall/recognition. Add at least one integrated vector reasoning item using input evidence, such as components -> magnitude/direction or same/opposite/parallel classification from arrows.
Do not reveal final resultant or total force as a diagnostic condition. Use evidence/workspace figures only.
requiredFixes: Keep DeepSeek-authored lessonAnalysis but update it if needed.
Use valid stemParts format: {type:'text', value:'...'} and {type:'math', latex:'...'} for formula/vector notation.
Rewrite figures to be renderer-safe, with non-empty answerLock.
Replace fig1 with a simple comparison_vectors diagram. Do not use cube_vector_edges for fig1. Use clearly separated arrows named u, v, w, z so that equal and opposite relationships are unambiguous.
No pair may appear in both answerLock.equalPairs and answerLock.oppositePairs.
Keep every diagnostic errorId mapped to reteach and retest.
Maintain Evidence Before Product: no diagnostic figure may be final_product.
